title: Like the look and fabric, but is runs a bit big, high rise
text:
I am:
125 lbs
5'3"
28" waist
38.5" hips

I ordered a size 7:
Front rise 9" (it fits like a high rise)
Hip front 18" (18x2= 39")
Length 31.5"

Fiber/material content:
Cotton 58%
Rayon 21%
Poly 18%
Spandex 3%

* Remember it will stretch and seem a bit bigger due to lots of poly and rayon; i like the poly and rayon, but will have to order a 5.
* In my experience about 1/5 inch larger than a 7.
* Prob won't shrink much due to lower content of cotton
* I REALLY like the feel...due to lower content of cotton
* If you like a soft feel, look for rayon, ramie, poly content
* If you like a thicker feel, look for high cotton content.
* In pictures shown here, I am wearing a thick fleece under the jeans. Only practical if I plan to wear thick fleece all the time. Even so the waist on the back side was loose.
* Really like the pocket detail stitching.
_gate_reason: The review describes fabric, fit, and specific measurements of the product.

title: Great Deal!
text:
I used to wear a pretty solid 10 in most jeans but along with the knowledge I've gained these last 3 years in grad school I've also gained 20ish pounds. Was time for new jeans. I didn't want to spend lots of money because once i graduate next spring and actually have time to take care of myself again, I'll be getting back into my 10s.

I tell you this all because figuring out what size to order was a process. I bought a couple pair of misses size 14s at thrift store, so i was going off that.

Junior sizes usually fit me a bit off as at 56 & 3 kids, I'm no junior. I ordered the 15s & crossed my fingers knowing with prime, returns would be easy peasy and free. They were too big. Not by much, but by end of day they were falling down and i had to put on a belt.

Ordered a 13. They came today. They are snug, but not uncomfortable by any stretch. I am pretty sure after i have them on for am hour they will be perfect.

The jeans have a nice stretch to them making bending and sitting comfortable (where was spandex in the 70s when i wore painted on bell bottoms as a teen?!?!).

The style is very similar to my much more expensive miss me jeans (that I'll wear again next year. Lol). I love the stitching and pockets. They are not uber low rise or uber high rise. They don't give me a big ol muffin top either. The pockets are deep enough to be functional.

I washed the 15s to see if they would shrink, but they only shrunk a bit in length. I'm about 5'7" tall and they are plenty long.

I don't have long term use to report, but if they last me a year, I'll be pleased. Besides, I'll be back in my 10s by then anyways, right?! :-)

I'll come back and update with photos soon.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' style, stitching, pockets, and fit.

title: Comfortable stretch fit but still durable. I love the back pockets! Great price!!!
text: OMG I love these jeans. I have ordered my second pair and have worn them both many times now. They are holding up well after having washed and dried them several times. They have remained very comfortable due to the stretch fit. This not a thin type fabric but a thicker more durable denim. The shrinkage is minimal, and at 5'2", I can wear them with platform wedges but they would be too long with flats. Before this, the only jeans I liked was Maurices, so these are much more affordable! The pockets on the back are great and help give the bottom a nice shape! Since I've had these a few months now, and they are holding up so nicely, I consider these jeans to be an excellent bargain and I highly recommend them.
_gate_reason: Describes the fabric thickness and fit of the jeans.

title: Dressy Jeans and very stylish
text: I normally wear a size 9 in most jeans, but when I purchased a size 9 in these, they were extremely roomy for my taste and I do not wear jeans skin tight by any means.  I love the look of these jeans though,  and the material is not too thick or too think.  The length is slightly longer than I would like.  I weigh about 116 lbs. and my height is only 5'3", so I would've had to hem these slightly, if I kept them.  When the size 9 arrived and I tried them on, I realized I'd need to go down a size, so I re ordered a size 7 and they fit me perfectly, even the length is perfect.  I may send the size 9 back or I may see if someone I know would like to buy them first.  I love the jeans, but you never know with sizing, every jean is made from different material, so you really have to consider that when ordering.  These jeans are 81% cotton, 18% polyester and 1% spandex, so they have some stretch to them with the lower cotton content.  For the price and free shipping, I consider theses jeans a most reasonable deal.  I would definitely buy these WallFlower jeans again.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' material, fit, and overall look.

title: Great, with drawbacks
text:
I've been looking for heavy stitch long jeans EVERYWHERE and I didn't think till after I'd spent an arm and a leg on a pair elsewhere to look on Amazon and then i was able to get 2 pairs for less than the price of the other one. (thankfully I can still return it)
There are some things I do not like about these, but this is standard dislike for most jeans for me. Since I've got a deep arch in my back all jeans gap and these do. A lot. I don't think I could size down, while it may fit around the waist the thighs are pretty tight as it is. They do have a nice stretch to them so they're not uncomfortable like other tight fit jeans I've had. I was hoping to wear these without a belt as the stitching around the waistband is a nice detail but I'll have to wear a belt because the gap is even more dramatic than on most jeans I have.
For size reference, I am 5'11" and my waist is 33"-34" and hips are 39" and I got a size 11.
Also, I got 2 pairs in Scarlett and Britney washes and they did vary in sizing by less than an inch here and there. Scarlett seemed a lot longer and Britney's didn't gap as dramatically.
_gate_reason: The review describes colors, washes, and details of the jeans.

title: Order a size smaller, and possibly the "short" size
text: I am 5'6", 180 lbs, average length proportions (torso and legs are average in length). I am normally a size 14 ladies but carry my weight in a bubble butt and large thighs. Unsure of what to expect I initially ordered a size 15 regular. They fit okay but I realized they are VERY stretchy and they are also very long. The 32" inseam of the "regular" is what I would normally find in a "long" in any other brand. Even with moderate heels (3") these would have dragged on the floor. I don't want to HAVE to wear 5-6" platform heels in order to wear these so I shipped them back and ordered a 13 short. These are great. The stretch accommodates my shape and the length is still below my ankle. I will be able to wear these with boots/heels/flats, anything. The style is great, the decorative stitching is nice. I like that the pockets (front and back) are functional and not fake. I am currently on a weight-loss journey and will buy these in smaller sizes (always in "short") as I go.
_gate_reason: The review describes the fit, length, and decorative stitching of the pants.

title: FINALLY! THANK YOU!!!
text: I'm in my early 40s & have gained a few pounds so I needed some new pants that fit. I'm also petite, as in I have short legs & almost no torso. I need low rise Jean's or risk having them up on my ribs! I searched for-ever but they no longer make my favorite Levi's & the new styles are either high-waisted, skinny jeans, or jeggings. These jeans fit me perfectly!! They have a low rise without showing any plumbers crack (lol) & are boot cut so they go over the tops of boots, sneakers, whatever (which I love). I usually wear around a 14 but these come in odds so I ordered a 13. Couldn't have asked for a better fit! Snug in the butt & thighs and just regular everywhere else. The waist is a little tight but I expected that as I went smaller rather than have them too relaxed. Just enough stretch but not too much to where they say after an hour of wearing. Trust me, if any of these are your concerns, these are GREAT JEANS!!!!

title: Comfortable and cute
text:
I'm 5'4, about 130lbs, 26in waist, 34in hips, and I got the long inseam. I ordered a size 3 Long in the color Amy. They were very cute. The fabric is a thin cotton denim, feels comfortable and has a little flex in it. The belt loops were sewn in sturdy, and there is no gap in the waist. The size 3 on me is a little tight right out of the box, but I feel like it's the material fits your body better as you move around in it. I decided to return because this color blue isn't my favorite, it looks like a deep navy blue dye. I loved the stitching on the pockets.
Overall, the fit is good, the pants are sturdy, and the stitching is done well with good quality thread. 10/10 would get again. Great bang for your buck.

title: I wear 22's but these fit as 18's...
text: Very comfortable, fantastic amount of stretch, conforms well to curves without bunching or being overly constricting. Despite being too large for me, these pants don't leave those unflattering "loose" spots or that "saggy diaper" look. These pants are a great vibrant blue color and look super cute. Plus, you can't beat the price. I wear pants anywhere from size 18 to 22, usually longs, I even have a pair of 16 I occasionally wear. Long story short, women's jeans love to be  inconsistent with their sizing. For me, I ordered a size 20 but in hindsight it should have been a size 18. This is disappointing since the majority of my pants are 22's. The second reason I give these pants only 3 stars is because of length, they scrape to the ground which has never been an issue for any pants I've ever ownes. However, this works because I do wear cowboy boots on a regular basis so those boots provide enough lift to help with this issue. Still, that limits tennis shoes and flats. A smaller size would probably receive this issue, too. I would honestly give these jeans 5 stars if the sizing wasn't off. I'll order these again (in the correct size) in the near future.
_gate_reason: The review describes the vibrant blue color and fit of the pants.

title: Skip!
text:
Material quality: These jeans feel...cheap. And while I could try to blame that on the Lycra content which is meant to allow them to stretch, that doesn't hold up either. I pulled out a pair of comfort stretch jeans made by vanity jeans in the same size and length, and these are SUBSTANTIALLY lighter, the zipper even feels lighter, and the material is noticably thinner.

Length: The details says that the inseam on a "long" is 34". I measured the inseam of my pair and they came in at 33", which is a large difference when we're talking about inseams.

Color: The color is approximately as I expected from the photos. I would say these are true to Amazon's images

Fit: The length, obviously was a bit too short. The waist of these jeans hits above the hip, just below the belly button. Problem? They fit like an entire size too small. Seriously, I felt like a busted can of biscuit dough. Yeah, I got them to close, but they HAD to stretch to do that. They fit more like a size 9. I don't know if that is particularly the fault of the pants, because I have very wide hips, but I do know that every other pair of size 11 long jeans I own fit just fine (from multiple brands) and the rise and waist fit are identical.

I paid $28.99 for these and I printed a return slip within 5 minutes of receiving them. There is no way I would ever pay $30 for pants this cheap and thin that short you on the inseam and then don't fit in the waist.
_gate_reason: The review describes the color and fit of the jeans.

title: My new favorite jeans! :)
text: I have been wearing Angels Jeans in Junior sizes because women's jeans in the correct size are WAY too long for me. I am short (5'3") and curvy with a booty lol. So I have been sticking with Angels Jeans since I was in college or maybe even high school! They used to sell them at Walmart for like $15 a pair, and the last time I bought some, I got like 6 pair so they have lasted me at least 3 years with minimal wear (I work from home so I usually just wear leggings or yoga pants around the house). But I lost a few pair during my recent move and the last pair I did find have huge holes in them in the upper thigh area that are just not appropriate for someone my age. So I was searching for Angels Jeans here and instead found these Wallflower jeans in juniors sizes and the reviews all seemed to state they have a lot of stretch which is what I need with my constant up and down abdominal swelling from kidney related issues. So I took a chance and got two pair of these and they are AWESOME. They are a bit too long, but other than that, they are the exact same size as my Angels Jeans I love so much, AND they have even more stretch to them than ANY TOEHR jeans I have ever purchased. ::) They are really thin and I am not sure how long they will hold up, but for less than $20 a pair, you can't really go wrong here. And lemme tell ya, if you are a "curvy" girl with hips, thighs, and a big booty, then I am sure you know it's tricky to find jeans that don't push your booty down or flatten it out. These are perfect for those of us with curves that we actually wanna accentuate. :) I have a new fave and I will be ordering Wallflower jeans from now on. :) I hope they don't get crazy popular and raise their prices like Angels Jeans did though, or I would be super bummed out. Super comfy jeans and super affordable. :) So happy I decided to buy these jeans. :)
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' fit, stretch, and suitability for curvy body types.

title: Loose Stitching and Strings All Over
text:
I am 5'11" and a size 4, with a very straight figure.

Following the Junior size chart, I ordered these jeans in a size 7. And these are going back.

The fit was OK, although I definitely wish they were more fitted in the hips/thighs. Sizing down would not have worked. The jeans are cut for someone with more curves than I have.

The inseam was the advertised 35". The length was great.

I do not care for the back pocket placement. They were lower than I expected and they were not straight or even. It was not flattering.

Pocket alignment aside, the jeans are very poorly constructed. There was loose stitching and strings all over. I put my hands in the front pockets and got a handful of lint in each. I was afraid to pull out the lint, because I didn't want to snag a string and pull out the seams.

The looked like a sewing project.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' fit, inseam, and pocket placement visually.

title: Shocked they fit
text: Ok, I’m 52 years old and have had a child.  This momma should not be wearing Junior sized jeans (19 regular).  But I am.  And I love them.  My body shape is more like an inverted pear, very heavy across my chest and mid-section, with my butt and legs being proportionally narrower.  That being said, the junior 19 was tight around the waist, but fits my butt and thighs perfectly.  And the length is PERFECT, with a 2” heel they hit about 1/2” from the ground.  Just right, not too long where I’m walking on the hem and not too short, they don’t look like floods.  I originally bought a pair of 20w, but returned them because the legs were way too big on me and the waist and butt weren’t tight enough.  The junior sizing solved that problem.  I’ve suffered with that issue in most womens plus size jeans- if they fit around my mid-section my legs swim in them, so I’ve been banished to jeggings for a few years.  No more!  I can now relive my youth in 90s boot cuts.  Will post a pic and additional review once I wash them, wear them to see how they grow and look like a human.
_gate_reason: Describes fit, length, and body shape in relation to the jeans.

title: Katy Wallflower jeans
text:
The fit and color of these jeans are awesome! It's a challenge for me to find good fitting jeans since I have big hips, so I figured I'd try these ones. However, after three washes on the gentle setting & no dryer and wearing them only a few times, the inner seam by the zipper ripped open
:(
_gate_reason: The review mentions the fit and color of the jeans.

title: Poorly made! Wide leg not boot cut!
text: They fit well in the waist. Unfortunately, they are so big at the bottom (from mid-thigh down) they are not flattering at all. The leg opening at the bottom is so wide it's more like a flare/bell-bottom than a boot cut.The material is incredibly thin (not what you would want in a pair of jeans). I am 5'9 and the pants are so long, if I would wear them, I would have roll them up.  Overall I would NOT recommend these jeans. They are just not made right. I will be returning them.
_gate_reason: Describes the fit, leg opening, and material of the jeans.

title: Five Stars
text: Love these jeans!!  True boot-cut.  Fits nicely just below the waist.  Sharp pair of jeans.

title: In style with cutting the chill
text: Very nice fashionable piece to add to wardrobe. It is pure black and thin which makes good for wearing in style without being hot. It comes with two pockets on front plus buttons. Yes, will remove the chill.... Beautifully made and looks great....

title: Great fit for a curvy lady
text:
I'm in the process of losing a good bit of weight, and while I don't want to spend much money on clothes while I go down sizes, a well-fitting pair of jeans is a must for me. I tried these sight unseen based on the great price and Prime shipping. I am in LOVE. I am 5'3" and the short length is just right. There's a tiny bit of "give" in the denim but they don't stretch out and become too big during the day. Even though I'm still losing weight, I've ordered another pair in Scarlett (my first pair are in Britney) in the same size and am going ahead and ordering a pair a size down in Britney as well.

Friends noticed a) how much weight I had lost because these jeans were really flattering, b) they loved the exposed seams and light stitching on the seams and c) that they were a nice length.
_gate_reason: The review describes colors, seams, and fit of the jeans.

title: bell bottoms not boot cut.

Then I ordered 22 and 24 because I really needed new jeans and I don’t care what the number is. The 22s fit about the same as the 18s and the 24s I could barely zip up! I’ve included a picture comparison on 18, 22, and 24 to show that they are all essentially the same size. So frustrating!

Color is great! They have gone though the wash quite a bit and they still held there color. I love the pockets!!

title: pretty color and fit - order your usual size or up one
text:
My measurements:
height 5' 7.5"
weight 135 lbs.
36" hips
29" waist (& high waisted)
long legs

These are a pretty color and very nice fit. But I ordered the same size 5 that fit me perfectly in the other Wallflowers Luscious Curvy, and 5 was too small in these!

I think that these jeans are sized correctly, and the Luscious Curvy should have been labeled smaller. The Luscious Curvey are so stretchy that you have to order a size smaller, but these are not stretchy at all, so order these in your usual size (or MAYBE 1 size up).

Regarding length, these are pretty long. I have long legs and usually require size Long, but these were plenty long enough in Regular (in both styles). I discovered this when I originally ordered the Luscious Curvy in Long and it was WAY too long. Returned, and Regular was perfect. Only order Long if you have REALLY long legs.

Also, these are not nearly as soft as the Luscious Curvy. In fact, they're quite scratchy around the waist. I'm hoping they soften up after multiple washings because they're so pretty, I want to enjoy wearing them without red indentations all around my waist (or in between my waist & hip - they're not super-low-rise, but a good mid-rise).

I would recommend these jeans.
_gate_reason: Describes color, fit, length, and texture of the jeans.

Everyone comments on my butt in these...calling me bubble butt in a flattering way.

title: Boot Cut, Low Rise is Very Hard to Find at the Local Retailer
text: I just love these pants! They arrived quickly in a plastic bag, undamaged in any way. They are exactly what I was trying to find at the local retailer, but couldn't! They are low rise, boot cut. That is very hard to find lately with all the other types of jeans on the market (eg: skinny, boyfriend cut, etc.) I ordered a size 9 for my 5'6", 139 lb frame. They are long. Very long. And if I didn't plan on wearing them with a western wear boot that has a heel, I would seriously consider having them professionally altered. The inseam has to be at least 32". When you think of ordering, check on the inseam length, because I think there was a shorter version in the same size. But I'm keeping them. They fit like a glove.
_gate_reason: The review describes the pants' cut, rise, and fit details.

title: seriously legit
text: I ordered these because I love LA Idol jeans and how they fit me. I am 5'7 and weigh 175. My inseam is 34. I have ordered LA Idol jeans for years. And since they are more well known now, they have gone up in price drastically. So, on a whim, I thought I would try these. The price was right and seemed like others approved. I cannot tell you how much I LOVE these jeans! The style is not cheap looking at all. I went through my bling on the pocket phase and really am out of it. I also bought the Laura which has a little more of a decorative flap. I was worried the flaps would be too small but they aren't. The length is perfect. I can wear heels with them. I have a bigger booty, so most jeans are too big in the waist and too tight in the booty so I always have to wear a belt or you see my underwear! These jeans fit my waist so well that I think a belt is optional. They have just the right stretch to them to accomodate booty room as well. My thighs are not on the big side but I have gained weight so opted for the size 13 and they fit really well! I highly recommend these jeans!!!!

title: Perfect
text: Its hard to find flattering plus size clothes.  Normally jeans either dig into my stomach where I cant sit down or they are too loose at the stomach and roll when I sit down, etc.  The bottoms of most jeans I buy tend to roll up (which I hate).  I have not been able to find a good pair of jeans that flattered my figure.  Its also hard to find jeans in my size that don't look like grandma jeans.  However, these are perfect!  The fit is perfect.  It is about as flattering as it gets on a plus size girl.  I immediately look slimmer.  Also, it has a nice style to it so it doesn't look like grandma pants.  I also feel the need to say these jeans do not give you a camel toe.  That's another problem lots of plus size girls have.  They don't roll up at the bottom or roll down when I sit.  They are just perfect.  I don't think Ill ever buy another brand of jeans again.  I also LOVE the flare version of this jean.  Got both in on the same day.  Love them!  I know its hard to buy without trying it on first but take a gamble with these.  You will be so glad you did. :)

title: I Got My Hourglass Shape Back!
text: I recently lost about 65 pounds and needed some new jeans.  I still have more weight to lose so I wasn't wanting to buy a whole new wardrobe, just one pair of jeans to wear without looking like a hobo until I can get closer to my goal weight.  These had good reviews and a good price so I gave them a try and I am glad I did.  They are so comfortable!  Now, keep in mind that I have been wearing jeans that are several sizes too big so to put on a pair of jeans that I am not constantly hiking up was a big plus in my book.  But the comfort level in these was way beyond that.  I mean, I could sleep in these.  The material is soft and has a nice stretch to it but not in a cheap way.  The first time I wore them was to meet my husband and realtor to look at houses.  My husband was like "wow!  you're really getting your hourglass figure back" and my realtor, who I hadn't seen for only a couple of weeks, said "you look great...  are you losing weight?"  I don't know about you, but for the price of these jeans, I'll take those compliments all day long!  I do agree with some of the other reviews - the boot cut is much more of a flare cut and the jeans are a little long on me.  (I am 5' 8-1/2" and I ordered a size 22.)  For this reason the jeans definitely need to be worn with a substantial shoe rather than a flimsy flip flop.  But don't pass these up for that reason.  Seriously, I love them.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' material, fit, and cut details.

I like that the crotch area is smaller so you don't have all that extra material gathering up there. It is a wider boot cut, some may see them more as bell bottoms, but I like them wider. All around great jeans (will definitely buy more of them!). Great price too!

title: Very disappointing
text:
Sadly, these are not the apple shaped holy grail I was led to believe in the reviews.

I purchased this pair and 2 of the other style all in the exact same size and all 3 wildly varied in waist, leg circumference and length. One pair was 2 inches longer and the leg circumference was over 2 inches larger than the other two. They were like large trouser pants, not bootcut at all. After seeing all of the glowing reviews I was so excited to try these jeans and this pair was the worst of the three.

This one came with only 1 tag so maybe a return? The material at the waist band was very thin and scratchy. The threads on the back pockets were fraying like crazy as well as some of the seams. The edges of the flaps on the back pockets were sticking up. The front pockets go all the way to the zipper. It’s bizarre.

Besides all that, they are pretty high waisted as well. I don’t necessarily mind that but because this is thin, cheap material, it’s not comfortable at all. If you have weight in your belly then what happens is the waist cuts you off in a weird spot and because there’s no support on top of extra room, it makes your belly stick out even more below the waist. It’s just not a good look.

It feels like I’m an island here but I would never pay this much for these jeans. The pair that was a lot bigger had the nicest fabric of the 3 but after being burned 3 times, I’m done ordering more hoping that finally I’ll get a good fit. I will stick to Silvers instead.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans' material, fit, and visual issues with pockets.

text: I bought the Keller style in size 3 short. It's got a nice styling with the embroidered back pockets, but the fit was a little off for me, so I had to return it. I'm 5'6" and a usual size 5 in junior jeans. Based on other similar styles that I've tried, I felt my usual size 5 was always too roomy at the thigh and long, so I experimented with going one size down and short length. The size 3 short fit perfectly everywhere except for the waist. It was just the right length inseam, the thigh fits good. The waist was a tight fit. If the fabric had more stretch, it might've been ok. But as it was, this style's fabric didn't have a ton of stretch in it to begin with. So, it's a no-go for me on the Keller style.

title: Cute and stretches a lot
text:
A little slow on the arrival time but not a horribly long wait. I like that these jeans are for a regular sized woman. They fit perfectly when I tried them on. I still decided to exchange them for a smaller size because they do stretch nicely and take into account my need for more room through the hip and thigh area. When I did the exchange, I went ahead and purchased THREE other pair. These jeans are just that cute and comfortable. Three of the four arrived, so I washed them. They didn’t shrink or fade. After wearing them a little while, I became disappointment though. They quickly became WAY too loose, and I’m a person who prefers a looser fit. However, I despise having to either pull up my pants all the time or get stuck wearing a belt. So even though I ordered a size down, I should have ordered TWO sizes down to take into consideration just how much they stretch. Once the last pair arrives, I can at least exchange it for the smaller size. It’ll make three new pair that require wearing a belt and only one to fit like I had expected. If I hadn’t washed them all, they definitely would be sent back for exchanges. Obviously, I can’t. 😣
Bottom line, cute, comfy, and a lot of stretch. Did I say they stretch?!? I still recommend buying these jeans; just know that they don’t shrink and stretch a lot after you wear them for a while.
_gate_reason: The review describes the jeans as cute, comfortable, and stretchy.

title: Very high-rise, odd fit.
text:
I bought two pairs of jeans from this brand. They were the same size, 20L. I am 5'10" tall. The length on them is awesome. That is the one good thing I can say. They also look nice, color wise.
However, the sizing is not consistent and their descriptions of the jeans are not accurate. This pair was supposed to be mid-rise (which is why I bought them) and they are so high they come up higher than my waist, or I have to wear them with the crotch baggy.
The waist band is also weirdly tighter than the rest of the pants. The pants would be comfortable but the waist band sits in an uncomfortable spot and is the tightest part of the pants. If the waist band had a touch of stretch like the rest of the pants it would be fine, but it does not stretch at all and just digs in to my stomach when I sit down or lean over.
The other pair I bought were really baggy and were falling off, even though they're the same size. They were also not the mid-rise they were advertised to be.
I will be avoiding this brand of jeans in the future.
_gate_reason: The review describes the color and fit of the jeans.
